9 - How To Maintain Growth And Power.mp4
So how to maintain growth and power? Simple, one word. Consistency. Your ability to take consistent action, your ability to do the things that, like I said, you may not want to do every day, that you need to do, that you probably aren't going to do because you don't have the mental discipline in order to keep yourself on track to take consistent actions, right? Because let's be honest, you've probably gone to an event or maybe you've gone to one of those online seminars or online webinars where all of a sudden, oh boy, you are just peaked. You are through the roof. Your motivation levels, your action levels, all of it are just absolutely through the roof. But...
that is day number one, and then comes day number two, and you're like, 'All right, sweet—I'm gonna go take a bunch of action, and I'm gonna do all those things that I learned at the event yesterday And, you know, you're still feeling really motivated. You're still feeling really good. And then day three comes, and like, you know, your mom calls and whatever else happens. It's pretty normal stuff. And like, you know, your motivation goes down a little bit. You're not doing as much. As you did the days before, because you know you simply don't have the same people around you and you're not in the Facebook group and you're not opening the emails, so you know when day four comes. Not only are you a little lower, but you're also a lot less motivated.
But then, day five, when the email finally does come, instead of going from zero motivation, you decided to bump it up a little bit that day. But oh man, they really messed up because you did not fully embrace everything that you could have from that event. Because you've chosen—not to be consistent. You've chosen not to live in the data that you've exposed yourself to. You have to build a reality with all this truth that you're exposing yourself to. So when you want to level up, you've got to take your entire environment. You gotta mitigate the distractions. You gotta get rid of the stuff that isn't absolutely driving you— value every single day— and taking your stock through the roof, right?
If you're not consistent with your actions, if you're not able to be consistent with your actions, you have to change. Change is the only constant in life. Your ability to not convince yourself that things are the way they are— for whatever reason you think they are— is your ability to remain consistent with the things that you need to do. Every single day. No excuses. Absolutely nothing should stop you from the capacity to hit your goals dead on. With laser-focused accuracy and concentration. Because consistency... is the way that you maintain growth and power. And consistently level up no matter what level you're on, no matter who you are, no matter where you're at, no matter what you're doing in the world. Consistency in successful actions will breed successful results.
You're simply separated by those successful results coming into your life. Your life, because you are here where results do not exist and where you want to be where results do exist, is separated by the time. And the action. In between here and here. And so, with the two components— time and action— on your side, as long as you, day after day, continue to check the box, consistently take the actions. News your entire life and your entire set of statistics for measuring the results that you desire from getting the life that you want from you having the absolute positive power to maintain growth. And absolutely drive your stock value, your company. The results, the behavior. Influence you have over others around you in a positive light. Through the roof astronomically, because consistency after you leave that same event and applying the information that you learned while you're there, talking to the people that you met while you were there, joining into the Facebook groups, diving into the community, going to more events, consuming all of what you can that relates to that reality is more than important. It's critical because you have to know. But if you don't... you already saw how it goes and you're trying to level up. I'll see you in the next video.
